skill.name: managing-relationships
skill.description: Manage GitHub issue relationships with parent-child sub-issues, dependency queries, tracking links, and GitHub GraphQL operations through the gh CLI.
skill.summary: Manage GitHub issue hierarchies and relationship queries with GraphQL-backed commands.
skill.author: C0ntr0lledCha0s

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but manual review found no malicious intent or prompt injection. Most weak-crypto and Markdown execution detections are false positives; the real risk is authenticated GitHub GraphQL mutation through external gh commands.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated GitHub Operations Through External Commands
medium_findings.description: The script invokes the gh CLI with subprocess.run and uses the current authenticated GitHub session. This is legitimate for the skill, but it can modify issue relationships in the active repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are real and execute gh commands with fixed argument lists. The commands serve the documented purpose, but authenticated GraphQL mutations create an elevated operational risk.

medium_findings.title: GitHub GraphQL API Access
medium_findings.description: The skill relies on gh api graphql for repository queries and mutations. No evidence of third-party exfiltration was found, but network access to GitHub is required.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code and documentation directly reference gh api graphql. The endpoint is GitHub through the official CLI, so the finding is moderate rather than high.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Command Examples
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md external command detections are fenced bash and GraphQL examples for user guidance. They are not executable code inside the skill file itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are documentation code fences or inline command names. The file contains instructions, not runtime shell execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: GraphQL ID Fields Mistaken for Weak Crypto
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm detections map to GraphQL id fields, issue node identifiers, relationship names, and parser text. No evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found GraphQL relationship fields and display logic at the flagged areas. There are no cryptographic imports, hashes, ciphers, or password-handling routines in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Repository Metadata Queries
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance detections are repository and issue metadata queries needed for GitHub issue relationship management. No host enumeration, credential discovery, or local system profiling was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines relate to GitHub repository owner, repository name, and issue-parent checks. They do not inspect the host environment or collect unrelated system information.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated GitHub Operations Through External Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The script invokes the gh CLI with subprocess.run and uses the current authenticated GitHub session. This is legitimate for the skill, but it can modify issue relationships in the active repository.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are real and execute gh commands with fixed argument lists. The commands serve the documented purpose, but authenticated GraphQL mutations create an elevated operational risk.

content.value_statement: GitHub issue hierarchies and dependency links are hard to manage with the standard issue CLI. This skill uses GraphQL-backed commands to create, inspect, and verify issue relationships.
actual_capabilities: Add and remove GitHub parent-child sub-issue relationships.
List sub-issues and show completion progress for a parent issue.
Find the parent issue for a child issue.
Show blocking, blocked-by, tracked, and parent-child relationships.
Retrieve GitHub node IDs for issue numbers.
Provide direct GraphQL examples for advanced relationship operations.
limitations: Requires an authenticated gh CLI session with access to the repository.
Requires GitHub Issues and GraphQL API access for the target repository.
Blocking relationships are documented as read-only through this script.
GitHub allows only one parent issue per child issue.

use_cases.title: Build an Epic Hierarchy
use_cases.description: Create parent issues and attach related implementation tasks as sub-issues.

use_cases.title: Audit Issue Dependencies
use_cases.description: Review which issues block delivery and which issues are blocked.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Project Structure
use_cases.description: Move child issues to a new parent and verify the resulting hierarchy.

prompt_templates.title: List Child Issues
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check of one parent issue.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the managing-relationships skill to list sub-issues for issue 67 and summarize the progress.

prompt_templates.title: Create Parent Links
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the managing-relationships skill to add issues 68, 69, and 70 as sub-issues of issue 67, then verify the links.

prompt_templates.title: Move a Child Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the managing-relationships skill to move issue 68 under parent issue 100 and confirm the new parent relationship.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Relationship Graph
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the managing-relationships skill to show all relationships for issue 67, including parent, sub-issues, blockers, and tracked links.

output_examples.output: The skill returns the parent issue title, completion progress, and each child issue with its state.

output_examples.input: Add issue 68 as a child of issue 67.
output_examples.output: The skill confirms the child was added and prints the parent and child issue titles.
The skill lists issues that block issue 68 and issues that issue 68 blocks.

best_practices: Create all issues before adding parent-child relationships.
Check the current parent before using replace-parent behavior.
Verify relationships after each batch of changes.
anti_patterns: Using task lists when true GitHub sub-issues are required.
Creating deep hierarchies that are hard to navigate.
Running mutation commands before confirming the active repository.

faq.question: Does this skill create GitHub issues?
faq.answer: No. It manages relationships between existing issues.

faq.question: Does it need GitHub authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires the gh CLI to be authenticated with repository access.

faq.question: Can one issue have multiple parents?
faq.answer: No. GitHub supports one parent issue per child issue.

faq.question: Can it manage blocking dependencies?
faq.answer: It can display blocking and blocked-by relationships. The script documents blocking changes as read-only.

faq.question: Why does it use GraphQL?
faq.answer: GitHub relationship operations are available through GraphQL and are not covered by standard gh issue commands.

faq.question: Is it safe to run in any repository?
faq.answer: Use it only after confirming the active repository, because commands use your current gh authentication.
